title: Food Servers, Nonrestaurant
description: Serve food to individuals outside of a restaurant environment, such as in hotel rooms, hospital rooms, residential care facilities, or cars.

tasks.text: Place food servings on plates or trays according to orders or instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean or sterilize dishes, kitchen utensils, equipment, or facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor food distribution, ensuring that meals are delivered to the correct recipients and that guidelines, such as those for special diets, are followed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine trays to ensure that they contain required items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load trays with accessories, such as eating utensils, napkins, or condiments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take food orders and relay orders to kitchens or serving counters so they can be filled.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor food preparation or serving techniques to ensure that proper procedures are followed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Remove trays and stack dishes for return to kitchen after meals are finished.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Carry food, silverware, or linen on trays or use carts to carry trays.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record amounts and types of special food items served to customers.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Stock service stations with items, such as ice, napkins, or straws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare food items, such as sandwiches, salads, soups, or beverages.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Determine where patients or patrons would like to eat their meals and help them get situated.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Total checks, present them to customers, and accept payment for services.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
